info.title: APIEngine — Your Personal API Spec
info.description: Auto-generated OpenAPI 3.1 specification for your APIEngine account. Covers all 19 standard CRM objects, your custom objects, JSON DB, authentication, and quota/plan endpoints. Your api_namespace: 58693b18-a130-4c58-bc83-94968e507a44.

tags.name: Auth
tags.description: JWT-based authentication for APIEngine developer accounts.

auth_modes: none — these endpoints issue or verify tokens
x-codegen-hints.audience: APIEngine developers (your account at theapiengine.com). NOT for end-users of apps built on APIEngine — end-user auth is at /api/v1/sdk/auth/ and uses a different token type (AppUser JWT).
x-codegen-hints.typical_flow: POST /signup/ → (optional) verify-email → POST /token/ → use 'Authorization: Bearer <access>' on subsequent calls. Refresh via POST /token/refresh/ before access token expires.

tags.name: User Profile
tags.description: Read and update the authenticated developer's profile.

x-codegen-hints.key_field_note: api_namespace in GET /me/ response scopes all /api/v1/custom/ and JSON DB paths — cache it on app load.

tags.name: OAuth
tags.description: Browser-based OAuth flows (Google). Not callable from API clients.

x-codegen-hints.note: x-redirect-only: true on all operations — open in browser window, not fetch/XHR.

tags.name: Usage & Plans
tags.description: Subscription plans, quota counters, and feature-flag lookup.

x-codegen-hints.typical_flow: GET /api/v1/logs/plans/ → display pricing page → POST /api/v1/logs/subscribe/ with plan_uuid → GET /accounts/v1/api/auth/usage/ to hydrate feature flags.
x-codegen-hints.gating_pattern: Check plan_flags['<flag>'] from GET /usage/ before rendering features. All 403 responses from plan-gated endpoints have shape {detail: '<message>'}.

tags.name: Standard Objects — Bulk
tags.description: Cross-object bulk delete and bulk update for standard CRM records.

x-codegen-hints.note: Both endpoints accept up to 100 UUIDs per call. object_type must be one of the 19 standard CRM slugs. FK and auto-managed fields are blocked in bulk update.

tags.name: Standard Object Fields
tags.description: Tenant-scoped custom field schema definitions for standard CRM objects.

x-codegen-hints.soft_delete_note: DELETE is non-destructive — sets archived_at and a 30-day purge window. Use POST /{uuid}/restore/ to undo within the window.
x-codegen-hints.data_storage_note: Field values live in custom_data JSONField on each CRM record, keyed by field_name.

tags.name: API Logs
tags.description: Read-only access to ApiRequestLog entries — one row per authenticated API call. Use to power an API logs dashboard or to debug client integrations.

x-codegen-hints.separate_surfaces: Simulator traffic (/api/v1/simulator/ and /sim/) is NOT logged here. That traffic appears in a separate SimulatorRequestLog surface (to be documented in Phase 4). Production API traffic, custom-object operations, JSON DB operations, and all other authenticated calls ARE logged here.
x-codegen-hints.data_quality: latency_ms may be null on rows created before the Phase 3 middleware fix. ip_address reflects REMOTE_ADDR (proxy IP under load-balancer). user is null for unauthenticated requests.

tags.name: Analytics
tags.description: Aggregated statistics derived from ApiRequestLog. All endpoints respect plan.analytics_retention_days and cache responses.

x-codegen-hints.plan_minimum: Free (retention window varies: Free=7d, Starter=30d, Pro=90d, Enterprise=365d)
x-codegen-hints.caching_note: Responses are cached per user+days. Pass ?force=1 to bypass cache. Don't compute analytics from /api/v1/logs/ — use these endpoints instead.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Webhook configuration, delivery logs, test firing, and internal automations. All operations require plan.can_use_webhooks=true.

x-codegen-hints.delivery_note: Webhooks fire on record-level events across standard objects, custom objects, and JSON DB. Use the events field on WebhookConfig to subscribe to specific event types. Retry behavior gated by plan.webhook_retry_enabled.

tags.name: SDK Auth
tags.description:
Authentication flows for end-users of apps built on APIEngine. Different from developer auth (`/accounts/v1/api/auth/*`).

Every request must include `X-API-Key: <your_tenant_api_key>` (from Developer Settings → SDK tab). This identifies *which APIEngine project* the end-user belongs to. End-users are isolated by tenant — alice@example.com signing up to your hackathon site is a separate AppUser from alice@example.com signing up to a different APIEngine customer's app.

After login, all subsequent requests must include both `X-API-Key` (to identify the tenant) and `Authorization: Bearer <appuser_jwt>` (to identify the end-user within that tenant).

Emails (verification code, welcome, password reset) are sent via APIEngine's SMTP infrastructure but branded with your tenant's display name (set in Developer Settings → from_name).

x-codegen-hints.plan_minimum: Free — no plan gate on SDK auth endpoints
x-codegen-hints.audience: End-users of apps built on APIEngine. NOT for APIEngine developers themselves — those use /accounts/v1/api/auth/*. If you are scaffolding an app that has its own user base (e.g. a SaaS, marketplace, or platform), these are the endpoints to use for that user base.
x-codegen-hints.tenant_isolation: AppUsers are scoped to a tenant. The X-API-Key header determines which tenant's user base you are operating on. Two AppUsers with the same email but different tenants are separate identities. unique_together constraint: (tenant, email).
x-codegen-hints.token_distinction: AppUser JWT is NOT interchangeable with developer JWT. Do not use a developer JWT on /sdk/auth/me/ — it will fail (wrong token type claim). Do not use an AppUser JWT on /accounts/v1/api/auth/me/ — it will also fail. The type claim in the JWT payload distinguishes them: 'app_access' vs simplejwt default.
x-codegen-hints.typical_signup_flow: 1. POST /signup/ → user created, 6-digit verification code emailed (template: sdk_verify_email.html). 2. User enters code in your UI. 3. POST /verify-code/ with {email, code} → returns access + refresh tokens + user. 4. Use access token as Bearer on subsequent calls. 5. GET /verify-email/{token}/ (legacy) — browser redirect link in same email, also verifies and redirects to {FRONTEND_URL}/login.
x-codegen-hints.typical_forgot_flow: 1. POST /forgot-password/ → email sent to user (template: sdk_password_reset.html). 2. Email contains link: {FRONTEND_URL}/reset-password?token={reset_token}. 3. Frontend extracts token from URL query param. 4. POST /reset-password/ with {token, password} → password updated. 5. User calls POST /login/ to get new tokens.
x-codegen-hints.token_lifetime_note: Access token TTL: 15 minutes. Refresh token TTL: 7 days. POST /token/refresh/ returns only a new access token — the refresh token is NOT rotated. Store refresh token in secure storage (httpOnly cookie or secure native storage).
x-codegen-hints.no_oauth_yet: OAuth providers (Google, GitHub, etc.) for SDK Auth are not yet implemented. Only email + password. Roadmapped for Phase 4b.
x-codegen-hints.email_branding: Emails use tenant.display_from_name as the sender display name. Set from_name via PATCH /api/v1/developer/settings/ to brand emails sent to your users.

BearerAuth.description: Developer JWT access token. Obtain via POST /accounts/v1/token/. Represents an APIEngine developer account. NOT interchangeable with AppUserBearerAuth.

ApiKeyAuth.name: X-API-Key
ApiKeyAuth.description: Tenant API key from Developer Settings → SDK tab. On SDK Auth endpoints this identifies which APIEngine project (tenant) the end-user belongs to — not the developer themselves.

AppUserBearerAuth.description: AppUser JWT obtained from POST /api/v1/sdk/auth/login/ or POST /api/v1/sdk/auth/verify-code/. Scoped to a single tenant and represents an end-user of an app built on APIEngine — NOT an APIEngine developer. Uses the same signing key as BearerAuth but carries a different claim (type: 'app_access') that rejects developer tokens and vice-versa. Access TTL: 15 minutes. Refresh TTL: 7 days.

Error403.description: Permission denied. Common messages: 'No active subscription.', 'API call limit reached for your subscription plan.', 'Record limit reached for your subscription plan.', 'You do not have permission to perform this action.'

detail.example: API call limit reached for your subscription plan.
Request was throttled. Expected available in 1 second.

refresh.description: Long-lived JWT. Use to obtain a new access token before expiry.

x-codegen-hints.call_sequence_note: Store refresh token in secure storage. Call POST /token/refresh/ before access token expires.

username.description: Optional. Auto-derived from email local-part if omitted (sanitised to [a-z0-9_], max 28 chars).

api_namespace.description: Embed in all /api/v1/custom/ and JSON DB paths.

fk_fields.user.api_namespace: Use this UUID in all /api/v1/custom/{namespace}/ paths and JSON DB record URLs.

fk_fields.api_namespace: UUID that scopes all /api/v1/custom/ and JSON DB paths. Read-only.

email.description: Changing email triggers a new verification email.

PlanSummary.description: Available subscription plan. All fields come from Plan model via PlanSerializer(fields='__all__').

price.description: Legacy USD display price. Do not use for billing — see price_inr_paise.

x-codegen-hints.currency_note: price_inr_paise is the canonical billing field (INR paise = rupees × 100). price is a legacy USD display string — do not use for billing logic.
x-codegen-hints.plan_gate_notes: allow_update=false → PATCH on CRM records returns 403. allow_delete=false → DELETE on CRM records returns 403. burst_rate_per_second controls PlanBurstThrottle; 0 = unlimited.

SubscriptionDetail.description: Active subscription with inline plan summary and current-month usage counters.

anyOf.description: Subset of PlanSummary fields included in subscription response.

plan.description: null when user has no subscription (Free tier).

in_grace_period.description: true if subscription expired but within 7-day grace window.

x-codegen-hints.canonical_usage: Use plan.api_calls_per_month and usage.api_calls_used to render a quota progress bar. Gate feature UI on plan.can_use_* flags.

UsageResponse.description: Current-month counters + plan quota limits + all feature flags. Returned by GET /accounts/v1/api/auth/usage/. All plan_* quota fields are null when user has no active subscription.

plan_flags.description: All plan feature flags as a flat {flag_name: bool} dict. Keys include: can_use_simulator, can_use_webhooks, can_use_json_database, allow_email_api, allow_pdf_api, allow_file_storage, allow_sms_api, allow_team_members, allow_update, allow_delete, and others.

x-codegen-hints.canonical_usage: Gate every feature flag in your app against plan_flags['<flag_name>']. Use api_calls / api_calls_per_month for a quota meter.

JsonRecordCreate.description: Send any JSON object. Metadata keys (name, category, description, tags) are extracted as first-class fields; everything else is placed into 'data'.

JsonRecordUpdate.description: Partial update. Metadata keys update their fields; all other keys are deep-merged into the existing 'data' payload.

MetadataJsonRelationship.description: Required shape for LOOKUP and MASTER_DETAIL field types. Enforced server-side.

related_object_type.description: 'custom' for user-defined models; 'standard' for built-in CRM objects.

related_object_slug.description: Slug of the related model. For 'custom': must match an existing CustomModel slug you own. For 'standard': must be a valid standard object slug (e.g. 'accounts', 'contacts').

on_delete.description: Behaviour when the referenced parent record is deleted.

metadata_json.description: For LOOKUP and MASTER_DETAIL: must match MetadataJsonRelationship schema (validated server-side). For all other field types: any JSON object or omit entirely.
For LOOKUP and MASTER_DETAIL field types, must match MetadataJsonRelationship schema. For all other types, any JSON object is accepted.

access_type.description: public: anyone with the link. private: owner only. password: password-protected. email_otp: one-time code sent to email.

storage_pct.description: Usage as a percentage of limit (0–100).

FileAccessRequest.description:
Three sub-flows, selected by 'action':
- use_password: send {action, password} → access_token
- send_otp: send {action, email} → OTP emailed
- verify_otp: send {action, email, otp} → access_token
Use the returned access_token as ?token=<value> on the download URL.

access_token.description: Opaque token; pass as ?token=<value> on the download URL. Valid for 1 hour.

password.description: Required when changing access_type to 'password' without an existing password.

body.description: Email body. Interpret according to body_format.

body_format.description: html: send as-is. markdown: converted to HTML with styling. text: plain text only.

data.description: Key-value pairs for {{placeholder}} variables in the template. Values are cast to strings.

emails.description: Most recent 200 emails in the window.

password.description: Stored encrypted at rest. Never returned in responses.

from_email.description: Verified sender address used in the From header.

object_type.description: Slug of the object type to delete records from.

uuids.description: UUIDs of records to delete. Max 100 per request.

object_type.description: Slug of the object type to update.

uuids.description: UUIDs of records to update. Max 100 per request.

fields.description: Field key-value pairs to apply to all matching records. Blocked fields: uuid, created_by, created_at, updated_at, creation_date, account_creation_date, user_creation_date.

user.description: Django User pk of the calling developer. null for unauthenticated requests.

token.description: Authorization header value (Bearer token prefix included).

ip_address.description: REMOTE_ADDR at time of request. Under load-balancer deployment this reflects the proxy IP, not the client IP.

latency_ms.description: Round-trip latency in milliseconds. May be null on log rows created before the Phase 3 middleware fix was deployed.

response_size.description: Response body size in KB (rounded to 2 decimal places).

request_id.description: Value of the X-Request-ID header, if supplied by the caller.

ApiRequestLog.x-data-quality-note: latency_ms may be null on rows created before the Phase 3 middleware fix (_start_perf was set after request.body read; could be skipped on client-disconnect). ip_address is REMOTE_ADDR — proxy IP under Render/load-balancer deployment; X-Forwarded-For parsing fix is on the roadmap. user is null for requests not attributable to an authenticated developer account.

success_rate.description: Percentage of successful requests (0.0–100.0). 0 when total_requests is 0.

avg_latency_ms.description: Mean latency in milliseconds. Excludes rows with null latency_ms.

p95_latency_ms.description: 95th-percentile latency in milliseconds. PostgreSQL uses percentile_cont; SQLite falls back to Python sort.

unique_endpoints.description: Count of distinct path values in the window.

plan_max_days.description: Maximum retention days allowed by the active plan.

x-codegen-hints.note: Cache key includes user + days. Use ?force=1 to bypass cache for debugging only.

path.description: Normalised path — UUIDs and numeric segments replaced with {id}.

avg_ms.description: Mean latency in milliseconds for this endpoint.

headers_json.description: Extra headers sent with every delivery.

payload_template.description: Optional JSON template string. Use {field_name} placeholders from the record. If empty, the default payload is {event, object_type, record}.

events.description: List of event types to subscribe to.

object_types.description: Optional: restrict to specific object types (e.g. ['accounts', 'contacts']). Empty = all objects.

secret_key.description: Optional secret forwarded as X-Webhook-Secret header on every delivery. NOT HMAC-signed — raw value only.

notify_on_failure.description: Send email alert when all delivery attempts are exhausted.

x-codegen-hints.signing_warning: secret_key is forwarded as a raw X-Webhook-Secret header. HMAC-SHA256 signing is NOT implemented. Ensure your endpoint validates the secret value over HTTPS only.

status_code.description: HTTP status code from the target server. null on network timeout or connection error.

error.description: Error message if delivery threw an exception (e.g. 'Request timed out (10s)').

max_attempts.description: Total attempts planned (1 = no retry, 3 = 2 retries). Retry gated by plan.webhook_retry_enabled.

x-codegen-hints.retry_note: max_attempts > 1 only when plan.webhook_retry_enabled=true. Retry delays: 30s then 300s.

action_config.description: For create_standard_record: {object_type, fields}. object_type must be a valid standard CRM slug. fields values may use {placeholder} syntax referencing trigger record fields.

x-codegen-hints.note: Unlike WebhookConfig (which POSTs to external URLs), automations execute internal APIEngine actions.

WebhookTestResponse.description: Response from POST /webhooks/<uuid>/test/. Returns the latest WebhookLog row if the test delivery completed within 12 seconds; otherwise returns a detail message.

detail.description: Fallback message when log row is not yet available.

x-codegen-hints.note: success=false with error='Request timed out (10s)' means the target URL didn't respond within 10 seconds.

AppUser.description: An end-user of an app built on APIEngine. Distinct from the User schema (APIEngine developer accounts). Scoped to one Tenant; same email can exist in multiple tenants as separate AppUser records.

id.description: Stable UUID for this AppUser. Use as a foreign key in your own data.

email.description: Unique within the tenant. Stored lowercase.

display_name.description: '{first_name} {last_name}'.strip() or email username if both are blank.

email_verified.description: False until user completes POST /verify-code/ or GET /verify-email/{token}/.

x-codegen-hints.audience_note: This is an end-user of an app built on APIEngine. Distinct from the User schema (which represents APIEngine developers). Do not pass a developer JWT when this schema is expected.

fk_fields.tenant_id: Tenant UUID — the APIEngine project this user belongs to. Set automatically from X-API-Key on creation; never returned in API responses.

x-codegen-hints.filter_fields: Not directly queryable via SDK Auth endpoints. Developer-side admin queries: GET /api/v1/developer/app-users/ (Phase 4c).
x-codegen-hints.custom_data_note: AppUser has no custom_data field. Store app-specific metadata (role, plan, profile) in your own data store using app_user.id as the foreign key.

AppTokenPair.description: JWT token pair returned on successful login or email verification. access expires in 15 minutes. refresh expires in 7 days.

access.description: AppUser access JWT. type claim: 'app_access'. TTL: 15 minutes. Pass as 'Authorization: Bearer <access>' on protected calls.

refresh.description: AppUser refresh JWT. type claim: 'app_refresh'. TTL: 7 days. Use POST /token/refresh/ to mint a new access token. NOT interchangeable with the developer refresh token.

x-codegen-hints.token_type_note: These tokens carry type='app_access'/'app_refresh' in the JWT payload. The server rejects them on developer-facing endpoints and vice-versa.
x-codegen-hints.storage_note: Store access in memory (JS variable or state). Store refresh in httpOnly cookie or secure native storage. Never store access in localStorage — it is readable by any script.

AppTokenRefreshResponse.description: Response from POST /token/refresh/. Only a new access token is returned — the refresh token is NOT rotated.

access.description: New AppUser access JWT. TTL: 15 minutes.

x-codegen-hints.no_refresh_rotation: Unlike some OAuth implementations, the refresh token is not rotated on use. The same refresh token remains valid until it expires (7 days from issue).

x-codegen-hints.password_note: Minimum 8 characters. Stored as bcrypt hash (Django make_password). Never echoed back.

AppSignupResponse.description: Returned on successful signup (201). No tokens — user must verify email first.

detail.example: Account created. Please verify your email to continue.

email_verification_required.description: Always true on 201. Show a 'check your email' screen.

x-codegen-hints.no_tokens_note: Unlike some auth systems, signup does NOT return tokens. The user must verify their email via POST /verify-code/ or GET /verify-email/{token}/ before they can log in.

code.description: 6-digit numeric code from the verification email. Expires after 15 minutes.

x-codegen-hints.code_format: 6-digit zero-padded integer string (e.g. '047291'). Generated server-side and emailed to the user. Expires 15 minutes after issue.

x-codegen-hints.enumeration_safe: Response is always 200 {detail: '...'} regardless of whether the email exists. Safe to call without leaking which emails are registered.

token.description: URL-safe token from the reset link query param (?token=...). Expires 24 hours after issue.

x-codegen-hints.token_source: Extract from the reset link URL: {FRONTEND_URL}/reset-password?token=<value>. FRONTEND_URL is the env var you configure in your APIEngine deployment.
x-codegen-hints.expiry: Token expires 24 hours after POST /forgot-password/ was called.

x-codegen-hints.enumeration_safe: Response is always 200 regardless of whether the email exists. A new 6-digit code is generated and emailed if the account exists and is unverified.

x-codegen-hints.editable_only: Only first_name and last_name are writable. Other fields are read-only.

refresh.description: The AppUser refresh JWT obtained from /login/ or /verify-code/.

Forbidden403_PlanGate.description: Plan quota exceeded or subscription inactive. Common detail values: 'API call limit reached for your subscription plan.' (ApiUsagePermission), 'Record limit reached for your subscription plan.' (CanCreateRecord), 'No active subscription.' (no Subscription row).

example.detail: API call limit reached for your subscription plan.
You do not have permission to perform this action.

TooManyRequests429.description: Burst rate limit hit. Controlled by Plan.burst_rate_per_second via PlanBurstThrottle (SimpleRateThrottle). Staff and unauthenticated users are exempt.

example.detail: Request was throttled. Expected available in 1 second.

post.description: Exchange username + password for an access/refresh JWT pair. This is the primary login endpoint for developer accounts. Pass the access token as 'Authorization: Bearer <access>' on subsequent calls.

x-codegen-hints.canonical_usage: POST username + password → receive access + refresh tokens. Store refresh securely; use access as Bearer header.
x-codegen-hints.call_sequence_note: signup → verify-email (optional) → obtain-token → use Bearer. Refresh via POST /token/refresh/ before access expiry.

post.description: Exchange a valid refresh token for a new access token. Call before the access token expires to maintain a seamless session.

x-codegen-hints.canonical_usage: POST refresh token → receive new access token. Implement as a silent background call triggered ~30 seconds before access expiry.

post.description: Returns 200 if the token is unexpired and well-formed. Returns 401 otherwise. Useful for health-checking a stored token.

x-codegen-hints.canonical_usage: POST token string → 200 (valid) or 401 (invalid/expired). Use on app startup to check if a cached token is still usable before making real requests.

post.description: Create a new APIEngine developer account. Returns JWT tokens immediately — no separate login step needed. Username auto-derived from email local-part if omitted. A welcome email and admin alert are sent asynchronously.

x-codegen-hints.canonical_usage: POST email + password → receive access + refresh + user object including api_namespace. Store api_namespace — it scopes all /api/v1/custom/ paths.
x-codegen-hints.call_sequence_note: signup → (optional) verify-email → POST /token/ to re-authenticate on next session.

201.description: Account created — tokens + user info returned immediately

400.description: Validation error — email already registered or password too short

post.description: Triggers a password-reset email with a secure uid+token link. Always returns 200 regardless of whether the email exists — this prevents email enumeration attacks.

x-codegen-hints.canonical_usage: POST email address → user receives reset link. Follow up with POST /reset-password/ using the uid and token from the link.
x-codegen-hints.call_sequence_note: forgot-password → user clicks email link → reset-password with uid + token from link.

200.description: Always returned regardless of whether email exists

post.description: Set a new password using the uid + token from the reset email link. The token is single-use and has a Django-default 3-day expiry.

x-codegen-hints.canonical_usage: POST uid + token (from reset email) + new_password → password changed. Re-authenticate via POST /token/ after success.
x-codegen-hints.call_sequence_note: forgot-password → user clicks email link → reset-password → obtain-token.

uid.description: base64url-encoded user pk from email link.

token.description: Django PasswordResetToken value from email link.

post.description: Change password for the currently authenticated user. Requires the current password for verification. Sends a 'password changed' confirmation email after success.

x-codegen-hints.canonical_usage: POST current_password + new_password → password updated. Use when user-initiated from a settings page.

400.description: Incorrect current password or new password fails validation

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: Email verification token from the verification email.

get.description: Browser-facing endpoint. Validates the token and redirects to the frontend login page with ?email_verified=1 (success) or ?email_verified=0&msg=... (error). Not a JSON API — do not call from a typed client.

x-codegen-hints.canonical_usage: Open this URL in a browser — it redirects to FRONTEND_URL/login?email_verified=1 on success. Not suitable for API client calls.

302.description: Redirect to frontend with email_verified=1 or email_verified=0&msg=...

post.description: Re-sends the verification email for the authenticated user. Deletes any previous verification token and issues a new one.

x-codegen-hints.canonical_usage: Call when user requests a new verification email from the 'verify your email' banner.

get.description: Return full profile for the authenticated developer account. Includes api_namespace — the UUID required in all /api/v1/custom/ paths.

x-codegen-hints.canonical_usage: Call on app load to hydrate user context. Cache api_namespace — it never changes.

patch.description: Partial update for the authenticated developer's profile. Changing email triggers a new verification email and sets email_verified=false.

x-codegen-hints.canonical_usage: PATCH any subset of profile fields from a settings form. email_changed=true in the response means user must re-verify.

email_changed.description: true if email was changed and re-verification is required.

400.description: Username taken or email already in use

parameters.name: provider
parameters.description: OAuth provider slug. Currently only 'google' is wired.

get.description: Redirects the browser to the OAuth provider's authorization page. Not callable from a typed API client — open in a browser window/tab.

x-codegen-hints.canonical_usage: Browser redirect only — not callable from a typed client. Open as window.location.href or in a new tab.

302.description: Redirect to OAuth provider authorization page

get.description: Handles the OAuth provider redirect after user grants access. Creates or retrieves the developer account and redirects to the frontend with JWT tokens embedded in query params or fragment.

x-codegen-hints.canonical_usage: Browser redirect only — the OAuth provider calls this, not your client code. Extract tokens from the redirect URL on the frontend.

302.description: Redirect to frontend with auth tokens

x-codegen-hints.canonical_usage: Call on dashboard load. Use plan.* for feature gating; usage.* for quota meters. Check in_grace_period to show renewal warnings.

post.description: Subscribe to or upgrade to a new plan. Downgrading from a paid plan (sort_order decrease) is blocked — returns 400. Downgrades require contacting support.

post.summary: Sign up an end-user of your app
post.description: Creates a new AppUser account scoped to the tenant identified by X-API-Key. For end-users of apps built on APIEngine, not for APIEngine developer accounts. No tokens are returned on signup — the user must verify their email first (via POST /verify-code/ or the link in the verification email), then call POST /login/ to receive tokens. Emails the user a 6-digit verification code using template emails/sdk_verify_email.html, branded with your tenant's display name.

POST /verify-code/ with {email, code} → returns access + refresh tokens + user object. 4. Alternatively: user clicks link in email → GET /verify-email/{token}/ → 302 redirect to {FRONTEND_URL}/login.

post.summary: Verify email with 6-digit OTP code and receive tokens
post.description: Verifies the 6-digit numeric code that was emailed to the user after signup (or resend). For end-users of apps built on APIEngine, not for APIEngine developer accounts. On success, marks the AppUser as email_verified=True and returns JWT tokens — this is the preferred programmatic verification path. The code expires 15 minutes after it was issued. If the user is already verified, tokens are returned immediately (idempotent).

x-codegen-hints.call_sequence_note: Preferred post-signup flow: 1. Show a 'Enter your 6-digit code' input after signup. 2. POST /verify-code/ with {email, code}. 3. Store tokens, navigate to app home. Code TTL: 15 minutes from issue. Call POST /resend-verification/ to issue a fresh code.
x-codegen-hints.vs_verify_email: POST /verify-code/ is the programmatic path (6-digit OTP in your UI). GET /verify-email/{token}/ is the browser-redirect path (link in email). Both verify the same account; use /verify-code/ for mobile/SPA flows.

get.summary: Verify email via magic link (browser redirect)
get.description: Legacy browser-redirect endpoint. The verification email contains a magic link pointing to this URL. For end-users of apps built on APIEngine, not for APIEngine developer accounts. On success, marks the AppUser as email_verified=True and redirects to {FRONTEND_URL}/login?email_verified=1. On failure (token invalid or already used), redirects to {FRONTEND_URL}/verify-email?status=error. Does NOT return JSON — always returns an HTTP 302 redirect. Not intended to be called by API clients; it is embedded in the email link. Preferred programmatic alternative: POST /verify-code/.
